body.title: Fix github_sync.py set-github-issue subprocess hardcoded TheIgors path
body.result: Fixed hardcoded cc_queue path — now imports directly from ADC
body.description:
github_sync.py line 293 calls subprocess with hardcoded `~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py`. Since T-lab-migration-to-agent-datacenter, cc_queue.py now lives at ADC's lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py. The push-queue command creates GH Issues correctly but silently fails to write back the issue numbers via set-github-issue, so tickets accumulate no github_issue field.

Fix: replace the hardcoded path with a path relative to ADC repo, or import cc_queue directly and call cmd_set_github_issue() instead of spawning a subprocess.

**Affected files:** ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/github_sync.py (line 290-304)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: fix the subprocess path so set-github-issue writes back correctly. Out of scope: migrating all of github_sync.py to ADC (separate ticket), any other github_sync commands.
**Test plan:** Run push-queue on a test ticket, verify issue number written back to queue; no tests to add (integration-only path).
